# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-deployment
  namespace: development
  labels:
    app: my-application
    environment: development
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-application
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-application
        environment: development
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 10000
        runAsGroup: 10000
        fsGroup: 10000
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      containers:
        - name: my-application
          # SECURITY: Using a specific image digest instead of :latest tag.
          # The :latest tag is mutable and poses security risks:
          #   - No guarantee of what code is actually running
          #   - Susceptible to image tag overwrite / supply chain attacks
          #   - Makes rollbacks unpredictable
          #   - Breaks reproducibility across environments
          # Always pin to a specific digest (or at minimum a specific semantic version tag).
          # To get the digest: docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' myorg/my-application:latest
          image: myorg/my-application@sha256:abc123def456789exampledigestthatyoureplacewithactualvalue
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
              protocol: TCP
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            runAsNonRoot: true
            runAsUser: 10000
            runAsGroup: 10000
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL
            seccompProfile:
              type: RuntimeDefault
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "128Mi"
              cpu: "100m"
            limits:
              memory: "256Mi"
              cpu: "500m"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 5
          volumeMounts:
            - name: tmp-volume
              mountPath: /tmp
      volumes:
        - name: tmp-volume
          emptyDir:
            sizeLimit: "100Mi"
      # For pulling from a private Docker Hub repository
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: dockerhub-credentials
---
# RECOMMENDATION FOR DEVELOPMENT ENVIRONMENT:
# Instead of using :latest tag, set up automated image updates using one of these approaches:
#
# 1. Flux Image Automation Controller or ArgoCD Image Updater
#    These tools watch your container registry for new image tags/digests
#    and automatically update the deployment manifest in Git with the new
#    pinned digest, maintaining both automation AND security.
#
# 2. CI/CD Pipeline
#    Have your CI pipeline build, tag with a specific version (e.g., git SHA),
#    and update the deployment manifest with the new pinned reference.
#
# This way you get continuous updates WITHOUT sacrificing supply chain security.
